The described scheme (iteratively reweighted mean with inverse-variance weights) resembles IRLS, not Newton-Raphson on a score equation; this logical characterization is a central candidate for what 'check this' targets.","R1.3":"The 12% discrepancy-reduction figure and the 4-7 step convergence observation are concrete empirical claims anchored to a loosely described CIFAR-10H re-annotation experiment; alignment between these assertions and any underlying evidence is a plausible primary target of the delegation."},"clarification_needed":true,"clarification_question":"Is your primary goal to verify the mathematical correctness of the algorithm and its claimed properties (logical consistency, R1.2), rather than to assess whether the empirical performance claims are adequately supported by evidence (R1.3)?"}

rationale.R1.2: The artifact makes pointed mathematical claims—that the update constitutes Newton-Raphson iteration and inherits second-order convergence, and that no gradient computation is required. The described scheme (iteratively reweighted mean with inverse-variance weights) resembles IRLS, not Newton-Raphson on a score equation; this logical characterization is a central candidate for what 'check this' targets.
rationale.R1.3: The 12% discrepancy-reduction figure and the 4-7 step convergence observation are concrete empirical claims anchored to a loosely described CIFAR-10H re-annotation experiment; alignment between these assertions and any underlying evidence is a plausible primary target of the delegation.

parsed.clarification_question: Is your primary goal to verify the mathematical correctness of the algorithm and its claimed properties (logical consistency, R1.2), rather than to assess whether the empirical performance claims are adequately supported by evidence (R1.3)?
